title: Unit 3 Checkpoint Quiz — The Conditional Tense

questions.prompt: What are the conditional endings in Spanish?
options: -ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -ían (added to the infinitive or irregular stem)
questions.explanation: Conditional endings all contain -ía: -ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -ían. They're added to the infinitive (regular) or irregular stem (same stems as future).

questions.prompt: Which is the most polite request for a favor?
questions.explanation: '¿Podrías...?' (conditional) is more polite than '¿Puedes...?' (present indicative). The conditional creates social distance that reads as deference and courtesy.

questions.prompt: What is the yo conditional of 'salir'?
questions.explanation: 'Salir' uses irregular stem 'saldr-'. Yo conditional: saldría. Compare: future = saldré, conditional = saldría.

questions.prompt: 'Quisiera un agua, por favor.' What is this an example of?
options: A polite request using the conditional of querer
questions.explanation: 'Quisiera' is the yo conditional of 'querer' — it means 'I would like'. Using the conditional instead of 'quiero' (I want) makes requests significantly more polite.

questions.prompt: Complete: Si tuviera más dinero, ___ por toda Latinoamérica. (viajar)
questions.explanation: In if-clauses, the pattern is: Si + imperfect subjunctive (tuviera) + conditional (viajaría). The conditional describes what WOULD happen.

questions.prompt: The irregular stems for the future and conditional are:
options: Different — each tense has unique stems
The same as the preterite stems
Only irregular in the future, not the conditional
questions.explanation: The future and conditional share the same irregular stems: tendr-, vendr-, podr-, sabr-, har-, dir-, saldr-, pondr-, querr-, habr-. Learn them once, use them for both tenses.

questions.prompt: A waiter says '¿Le gustaría algo más?' What are they asking?
questions.explanation: '¿Le gustaría algo más?' uses the conditional of 'gustar' (to please/like) — 'Would you like anything else?' It's a standard polite service phrase in Mexican restaurants.

questions.prompt: What does 'Me dijo que vendría' mean?
options: He told me he would come.
He told me he will come.
He told me he used to come.
questions.explanation: The conditional is used in reported speech to express what someone said they WOULD do. 'Vendría' (conditional of venir) = would come. This is called the conditional in reported speech.
